name: Inland
description_short: Inland Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving the San Diego Catholic community since the 1950s, offering checking, savings, loans, and investment products.
description_long:
Inland Federal Credit Union has operated continuously since the early 1950s, with its official charter dated November 3, 1960. The organization is a member-owned, not-for-profit institution governed by an elected board of volunteer directors. Headquartered in La Mesa, California, it specifically serves the Catholic community in the San Diego area, though membership policies allow for broader eligibility.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including checking and savings accounts (with rates up to 2.5% APY on savings), auto loans (as low as 4.99% APR), personal loans (as low as 13.99% APR), home loans, credit cards, certificates of deposit, and IRA accounts. Members also have access to online and mobile banking platforms for account management, bill payment, fund transfers, and balance inquiries. The institution provides financial counseling services and emphasizes personal, courteous service.

Inland Federal distinguishes itself through its focus on community relationship-building and a "once-a-member, always-a-member" policy. The credit union participates in the CO-OP Network, providing members access to over 30,000 ATMs worldwide and 9,000 deposit-taking ATMs, including locations at 7-Eleven and Costco stores. This network access significantly exceeds typical single-institution ATM availability. The organization also offers insurance products including AD&D, life insurance, and auto insurance.

As a credit union, Inland Federal provides NCUA insurance protection on deposits and operates on a not-for-profit model designed to benefit members rather than external shareholders. The institution maintains limited physical hours (Monday-Friday, 9:30am-4pm) and requires new members to complete applications in person at the branch office, which may present accessibility challenges for some prospective members.
services: Checking accounts with online and mobile banking access
Savings accounts with rates up to 2.5% APY
Online banking with bill pay functionality
Insurance products (AD&D, life insurance, auto insurance)
pros: Access to 30,000+ ATMs worldwide including 9,000 deposit-taking ATMs at 7-Eleven and Costco locations, eliminating ATM fees for members
Competitive savings rates up to 2.5% APY and auto loan rates as low as 4.99% APR
Comprehensive financial services including checking, savings, credit cards, auto/personal/home loans, certificates, IRAs, and insurance products
Free mobile and online banking with ability to check balances, pay bills, transfer funds, and review transactions from any device
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure ensures profits benefit members rather than external shareholders
Professional financial counseling services available to help members achieve financial goals
"Once-a-member, always-a-member" policy provides stability and continued membership eligibility
Family membership option allowing multiple household members to join
cons: Limited branch hours (Monday-Friday 9:30am-4pm only) may be inconvenient for working members
New members must complete applications in person at the single La Mesa branch location, creating a barrier to remote membership enrollment
Personal loan APRs start at 13.99%, which is higher than many competitive personal loan lenders
Email communication is explicitly unsecured, requiring members to avoid sharing sensitive information electronically
No mention of 24/7 phone customer service; single phone number provided with limited business hours
best_for: Members of the San Diego Catholic community seeking relationship-based banking with local commitment
Consumers wanting to eliminate ATM fees through access to extensive nationwide and international ATM networks
Members needing comprehensive financial products (checking, savings, loans, insurance) from a single institution
Individuals who value not-for-profit credit union structure and member ownership over traditional banking
diagnosis: Inland Federal Credit Union is best for San Diego-area residents, particularly those in the Catholic community, seeking comprehensive banking and loan services with strong ATM network access and a community-focused, not-for-profit structure. The main caveat is the requirement to apply in person and limited branch hours, which restricts accessibility for remote applicants and those with unconventional schedules.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Inland — NCUA-insured credit union in La Mesa, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
